message:
test(ftui): add snapshot/golden suite for all migrated views across terminal sizes (FTUI-07.2)

Adds 31 full-frame snapshot tests exercising model.view() pipeline for
all 7 views (Home, Panes, Events, Triage, History, Search, Help) at
multiple terminal sizes (40x5, 80x24, 120x40) with populated, empty,
filtered, and degraded states. Includes frame_to_text() capture helper,
width-aware tab bar assertions, footer verification, and edge cases
(height<3, narrow/wide terminals). Also fixes LavenderGrove's render
call signatures after FocusRegion/cursor_pos additions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"wa-1pe.5","title":"Dry-run tests: verify no state modifications, output correctness, error paths","description":"
# Dry-Run Testing Suite

## Purpose
Ensure dry-run mode never modifies state and produces accurate predictions.

## Test Categories

### 1. No State Modification Tests
- Dry-run send: DB unchanged, no WezTerm CLI calls
- Dry-run workflow: No locks acquired, no step logs
- Dry-run approve: Approval state unchanged

### 2. Output Correctness Tests
- Target resolution matches actual resolution
- Policy evaluation matches actual evaluation
- Step plan matches actual execution order

### 3. Error Path Tests
- Invalid pane: helpful error in dry-run output
- Policy denial: shows what would block
- Expired approval: shows expiry clearly

### 4. Format Tests
- TTY output is human-readable
- JSON output is valid and complete
- All fields present in both formats

### 5. Contract Tests
- Dry-run output fields match execution fields
- No drift between preview and reality

## Testing Approach
- Mock WezTerm CLI to detect any calls
- Compare dry-run output to actual execution logs
- Snapshot tests for output stability

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 100% coverage of dry-run code paths
- [ ] Verified no state modification in any test
- [ ] Output snapshots for regression detection
- [ ] Error message quality verified

 {"id":"wa-1qr1","title":"FTUI-07.3.a Deterministic PTY fixture seed/timing strategy","description":"## Background
PTY E2E suites are only trustworthy if timing and input generation are deterministic and anti-flake by design.

## Deliverables
- deterministic seed policy for PTY scenario generation
- timing/control strategy that removes sleep-based nondeterminism
- anti-flake constraints (timeouts, retries, jitter controls, artifact requirements)

## Acceptance Criteria
- repeated runs produce stable outcomes and comparable logs across environments
- suite guidance specifies required transcript/log metadata for debugging
- strategy is adopted by FTUI PTY scenarios and referenced from CI gate bead.,","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:14:37.597376381Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor","updated_at":"2026-02-08T21:31:05.428526618Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","ftui","subtask"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-1qr1","depends_on_id":"wa-3gii","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:14:37.616084937Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor"},{"issue_id":"wa-1qr1","depends_on_id":"wa-bjvg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:25:34.127403507Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor"}]}

In-process output must obey one-writer constraints to keep rendering deterministic and avoid output corruption.

## Deliverables
- inventory and removal plan for direct stdout/stderr writes in UI-active paths
- unified output sink routing contract for logs/events/status lines
- verification checks to prove no bypass writes remain

- checks detect and fail on new bypass writes

 {"id":"wa-spi","title":"Watch-and-notify mode: wa watch --notify-only for alert-only monitoring","description":"# Watch-and-Notify Mode: Continuous monitoring with alert delivery

## Purpose
Enable wa to run in notification-only mode, alerting on events without auto-handling.

## Use Case
Some users want to be notified of events but handle them manually:
- Learning the system behavior
- High-stakes environments requiring human review
- Debugging automation issues

## Implementation

### CLI Command
```bash
# Watch and notify on all events
$ wa watch --notify-only

# Watch and notify only on specific events
$ wa watch --notify-only --notify-filter "usage_limit,compaction"

# Watch with specific notification channels
$ wa watch --notify-only --notify-via webhook,desktop
```

### Configuration
```toml
[notifications]
# Enable watch-and-notify mode
notify_only = true

# Which events trigger notifications
notify_filter = ["*.usage_limit*", "*.compaction", "*.error"]

# Notification channels
channels = ["webhook", "desktop"]

# Throttling
min_interval_seconds = 60
max_per_hour = 30
```

### NotifyOnlyMode Implementation
```rust
pub struct NotifyOnlyWatcher {
    event_bus: EventBus,
    notifier: Notifier,
    filter: EventFilter,
    throttle: ThrottleState,
}

impl NotifyOnlyWatcher {
    pub async fn run(&mut self) -> Result<()> {
        loop {
            let event = self.event_bus.recv().await?;

            if !self.filter.matches(&event) {
                continue;
            }

            if !self.throttle.allow(&event) {
                tracing::debug!("Throttled notification for {:?}", event.rule_id);
                continue;
            }

            // Log event without handling
            tracing::info!(
                event_id = %event.id,
                rule_id = %event.rule_id,
                pane_id = %event.pane_id,
                "Event detected (notify-only mode)"
            );

            // Send notification
            self.notifier.send(Notification {
                title: format!("wa: {}", event.rule_id),
                body: event.to_notification_body(),
                severity: event.severity(),
                actions: event.suggested_actions(),
            }).await?;
        }
    }
}
```

### Notification Body Generation
```rust
impl Event {
    pub fn to_notification_body(&self) -> String {
        format!(
            "Detected: {}\nPane: {} ({})\nTime: {}\n\nSuggested action:\n  {}",
            self.rule_id,
            self.pane_id,
            self.pane_title,
            self.timestamp.format("%H:%M:%S"),
            self.suggested_action().unwrap_or("No action suggested"),
        )
    }
}
```

### Desktop Notification Example
```
┌─────────────────────────────────────┐
│ 🔔 wa: codex.usage_limit_reached    │
├─────────────────────────────────────┤
│ Pane 9 (codex @ /project)           │
│ Detected: 14:30:15                  │
│                                     │
│ Run: wa workflow run handle_usage.. │
│                                     │
│ [Dismiss]  [Handle]  [View Details] │
└─────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Testing
- Notify-only mode does not auto-handle events
- Notifications delivered to configured channels
- Throttling works correctly
- Filter patterns match expected events

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] wa watch --notify-only implemented
- [ ] Event filtering via --notify-filter
- [ ] Throttling prevents notification spam
- [ ] Desktop and webhook channels supported
- [ ] Notification includes suggested actions
","notes":"Implemented notify-only mode for wa watch: CLI flags --notify-only/--notify-filter/--notify-via, config.notifications.notify_only, and channel/filter overrides in watcher startup; notify-only disables auto-handling while leaving notifications pipeline + cooldown/dedup in place. Added config field default + tests updated. cargo fmt/check/clippy/test passed.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"LavenderSnow","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T18:43:36.504048534Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-02-05T02:25:46.717143289Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T02:25:46.716981057Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-daemon","area-notifications","phase-4"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-spi","depends_on_id":"wa-psm","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:43Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"wa-sx5v","title":"FTUI-04.3 Implement deterministic UI state reducer and refresh scheduling","description":"## Background\nDeterministic state evolution is required for reliable snapshot/E2E testing.\n\n## Deliverables\n- reducer model for selection/filter/scroll state\n- refresh cadence policy and stale-data handling\n- predictable command/result state transitions\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- identical inputs produce identical state transitions\n- flake-prone timing behaviors are removed or bounded.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"LavenderGrove","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:08:02.465729914Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor","updated_at":"2026-02-09T02:04:06.139599619Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T02:04:06.139532434Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ftui","state"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-sx5v","depends_on_id":"wa-1ssn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:08:02.478934409Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor"},{"issue_id":"wa-sx5v","depends_on_id":"wa-3kal","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:17:00.140543292Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor"}]}
 {"id":"wa-t82","title":"[EPIC] Session Recording and Replay for Debugging and Sharing","description":"# [EPIC] Session Recording and Replay

## Mission
Record wa sessions for **debugging, sharing, and analysis**—replay exactly what happened.

## Why This Matters
When something goes wrong, the #1 debugging question is: "What happened?"

Session recording provides:
- **Debugging**: Replay the sequence that led to a bug
- **Sharing**: Show others exactly what wa did
- **Analysis**: Study patterns across sessions
- **Training**: Learn from real examples

## Components

### 1. Recording Mode
Capture all events and state during a session:
```bash
$ wa watch --record
Recording session to: /path/to/.wa/recordings/2026-01-18_143201.warecord
...
^C
Session recorded: 1234 events, 45 minutes

$ ls /path/to/.wa/recordings/
2026-01-18_143201.warecord
2026-01-18_150000.warecord
```

Recording captures:
- All pane output deltas (optional, configurable)
- All detection events
- All workflow executions and step logs
- All policy decisions
- Timestamps for everything

### 2. Replay Mode
Replay a recorded session:
```bash
$ wa replay /path/to/session.warecord

Replaying session: 2026-01-18_143201
Duration: 45 minutes (1234 events)

[Press SPACE to step, ENTER to play, Q to quit]

14:32:01 > Pane 1 detected: codex.usage_limit_warning
14:32:05 > Policy check: send allowed (rate: 2/10)
14:35:12 > Pane 1 detected: codex.usage_limit_reached
14:35:12 > Workflow started: handle_usage_limits
...
```

Replay options:
- Real-time (1x speed)
- Fast-forward (10x, 100x)
- Step-by-step
- Jump to timestamp

### 3. Recording Formats
```
.warecord    - Binary format (compact, fast)
.warecord.json - JSON format (human-readable, diffable)
```

Export options:
```bash
$ wa recording export session.warecord --format json > session.json
$ wa recording export session.warecord --format markdown > session.md
```

### 4. Session Annotation
Add notes to recording:
```bash
$ wa recording annotate session.warecord --at 14:35:12 --note "This is where it went wrong"
```

### 5. Recording Sharing
Share recordings safely:
```bash
$ wa recording share session.warecord --redact
Created: session_redacted.warecord
Removed: API keys, passwords, sensitive paths
```

## Privacy Considerations
- Recordings may contain sensitive data
- Redaction mode removes known secret patterns
- Warning when recording includes sensitive paths
- Recording retention policy (auto-delete after N days)

## Storage
- Recordings stored in `.wa/recordings/`
- Configurable retention (default: 7 days)
- Compression for old recordings
- Size limits per recording

## Testing
- Unit tests: Record/replay roundtrip
- Integration tests: All event types captured
- Privacy tests: Redaction removes secrets

## Success Criteria
- Recording captures all relevant events
- Replay accurately represents recorded session
- Redaction removes sensitive information
- Recordings are portable and shareable

 {"id":"wa-thl","title":"E2E test fixtures: reproducible terminal session recordings","description":"# E2E Test Fixtures: Reproducible terminal session data

## Purpose
Pre-recorded terminal output for deterministic E2E tests.

## Implementation

### Fixture Structure
```rust
// tests/e2e/harness/fixtures.rs

#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct OutputChunk {
    pub delay_ms: u64,
    pub text: String,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct ExpectedEvent {
    pub rule_id: String,
    pub required_metadata: HashMap<String, String>,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct TestFixture {
    pub name: &'static str,
    pub pane_title: &'static str,
    pub agent_type: &'static str,
    pub output_chunks: Vec<OutputChunk>,
    pub expected_events: Vec<ExpectedEvent>,
}

impl TestFixture {
    pub async fn replay(&self, harness: &E2ETestHarness, pane_id: u32) -> Result<()> {
        log_debug!(&harness.log_file, "Replaying fixture: {}", self.name);

        for chunk in &self.output_chunks {
            if chunk.delay_ms > 0 {
                tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(chunk.delay_ms)).await;
            }
            harness.inject_output(pane_id, &chunk.text).await?;
            log_debug!(&harness.log_file, "Injected: {:?}", chunk.text);
        }

        Ok(())
    }
}
```

### Built-in Fixtures

#### Codex Usage Limit
```rust
pub static CODEX_USAGE_LIMIT: TestFixture = TestFixture {
    name: "codex_usage_limit",
    pane_title: "codex @ /home/user/project",
    agent_type: "codex",
    output_chunks: vec![
        OutputChunk { delay_ms: 0, text: "Working on implementing the feature...\n" },
        OutputChunk { delay_ms: 100, text: "Adding tests for the new module...\n" },
        OutputChunk { delay_ms: 5000, text: "\n\n" },
        OutputChunk { delay_ms: 50, text: "You've reached your usage limit.\n" },
        OutputChunk { delay_ms: 50, text: "Daily limit resets in 4 hours.\n" },
        OutputChunk { delay_ms: 50, text: "\n" },
        OutputChunk { delay_ms: 50, text: "To continue, you can:\n" },
        OutputChunk { delay_ms: 50, text: "  - Wait for the limit to reset\n" },
        OutputChunk { delay_ms: 50, text: "  - Use a different account\n" },
    ],
    expected_events: vec![
        ExpectedEvent {
            rule_id: "codex.usage_limit_reached",
            required_metadata: hashmap! {
                "reset_hours" => "4",
            },
        },
    ],
};
```

#### Claude Code Compaction
```rust
pub static CLAUDE_COMPACTION: TestFixture = TestFixture {
    name: "claude_compaction",
    pane_title: "claude @ /home/user/project",
    agent_type: "claude_code",
    output_chunks: vec![
        OutputChunk { delay_ms: 0, text: "Analyzing the codebase structure...\n" },
        OutputChunk { delay_ms: 3000, text: "\n" },
        OutputChunk { delay_ms: 50, text: "⚠️ Context window nearly full. Compacting conversation...\n" },
        OutputChunk { delay_ms: 500, text: "\n" },
        OutputChunk { delay_ms: 50, text: "Context compacted. Some earlier details may be summarized.\n" },
        OutputChunk { delay_ms: 50, text: "Please re-share any critical context if needed.\n" },
    ],
    expected_events: vec![
        ExpectedEvent {
            rule_id: "claude_code.compaction",
            required_metadata: hashmap! {},
        },
    ],
};
```

#### Session Summary (Codex)
```rust
pub static CODEX_SESSION_SUMMARY: TestFixture = TestFixture {
    name: "codex_session_summary",
    pane_title: "codex @ /home/user/project",
    agent_type: "codex",
    output_chunks: vec![
        OutputChunk { delay_ms: 0, text: "\n" },
        OutputChunk { delay_ms: 50, text: "───────────────────────────────────────\n" },
        OutputChunk { delay_ms: 50, text: "Session Summary\n" },
        OutputChunk { delay_ms: 50, text: "───────────────────────────────────────\n" },
        OutputChunk { delay_ms: 50, text: "Session ID: sess_abc123xyz\n" },
        OutputChunk { delay_ms: 50, text: "Duration: 45m 32s\n" },
        OutputChunk { delay_ms: 50, text: "Tokens used: 125,432 / 150,000\n" },
        OutputChunk { delay_ms: 50, text: "───────────────────────────────────────\n" },
    ],
    expected_events: vec![
        ExpectedEvent {
            rule_id: "codex.session_summary",
            required_metadata: hashmap! {
                "session_id" => "sess_abc123xyz",
            },
        },
    ],
};
```

### Fixture Loading from Files
```rust
pub fn load_fixture_from_file(path: &str) -> Result<TestFixture> {
    let content = std::fs::read_to_string(path)?;
    let fixture: TestFixture = serde_json::from_str(&content)?;
    Ok(fixture)
}
```

### Fixture JSON Format
```json
{
  "name": "custom_scenario",
  "pane_title": "agent @ /path",
  "agent_type": "custom",
  "output_chunks": [
    {"delay_ms": 0, "text": "First line\n"},
    {"delay_ms": 1000, "text": "Second line after 1s\n"}
  ],
  "expected_events": [
    {"rule_id": "custom.event", "required_metadata": {}}
  ]
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Fixtures for all built-in patterns
- [ ] Fixture replay produces expected events
- [ ] Custom fixtures loadable from JSON
- [ ] Fixtures documented for contributors

## Testing
- Unit: JSON fixture load/parse and replay sequencing.
- Integration: replay fixtures through the harness and verify expected events.
- E2E: at least one scenario must use file-backed fixtures to validate determinism.

 {"id":"wa-tm0","title":"[EPIC] Phase 3: Full Agent Support","description":"# Phase 3: Full Agent Support

## Overview
This epic completes the agent-specific implementations for all three supported AI coding agents: Codex CLI, Claude Code, and Gemini CLI. After this phase, wa can fully monitor and automate all three agent types.

## Strategic Importance
The value of wa scales with the number of agent types it supports. Multi-agent orchestration requires consistent handling across agent boundaries. This phase ensures:
- Consistent pattern detection for all agents
- Workflow automation for all agents
- Browser automation for all auth flows
- Account rotation across all services

## Key Components

### 1. Complete Codex Patterns and Workflows
Patterns:
- codex.usage.warning_25 - "less than 25%"
- codex.usage.warning_10 - "less than 10%"
- codex.usage.warning_5 - "less than 5%"
- codex.usage.reached - "You've hit your usage limit"
- codex.session.token_usage - "Token usage: total=X input=Y ..."
- codex.session.resume_hint - "codex resume <uuid>"
- codex.auth.device_code_prompt - "Enter this one-time code"

Workflows:
- handle_usage_limits fully working
- Device auth flow fully automated
- Session resume tested end-to-end

### 2. Complete Claude Code Patterns and Workflows
Patterns:
- cc.compaction - "Conversation compacted"
- cc.session_start - "Claude Code v" (version detection)
- cc.usage_limit (evolves with Claude Code updates)

Workflows:
- handle_compaction fully working
- handle_usage_limits (if Claude Code exposes reset time)

Challenges:
- Claude Code's session identity is less clear than Codex's UUID
- May need cwd + start time correlation via CASS

### 3. Complete Gemini Patterns and Workflows
Patterns:
- gemini.usage.reached - "Usage limit reached for all Pro models"
- gemini.session.summary - "Interaction Summary"
- gemini.model.used - "Responding with gemini-"

Workflows:
- handle_usage_limits for Gemini
- Google auth flow via Playwright

### 4. Full Browser Automation
Complete the auth flows started in Phase 2:

#### OpenAI Device Auth (Codex)
1. Navigate to https://auth.openai.com/codex/device
2. Wait for and fill email
3. Handle authentication (password, OTP, or SSO)
4. Enter device code
5. Wait for success confirmation
6. Save profile state

#### Anthropic OAuth (Claude Code)
1. Navigate to console.anthropic.com/login
2. Handle Google OAuth or email/password
3. Complete any MFA
4. Persist session

#### Google Auth (Gemini)
1. Navigate to accounts.google.com
2. Complete Google login flow
3. Handle account selection
4. Persist session

### 5. Account Rotation Logic
- Track usage per account per service
- Select account with highest percent_remaining above threshold
- Round-robin fallback if all near limit
- Mark accounts as "cooling down" after hitting limits
- Notification when all accounts exhausted

## Agent-Specific Considerations

### Codex CLI
- Uses device code flow for auth
- Session ID is a clear UUID in resume hint
- Token usage clearly reported at session end
- "cod" command prefix (verify actual binary name)

### Claude Code
- Uses browser-based auth
- Session identity less obvious (cwd + time?)
- Compaction is the primary automation target
- Version banner at session start

### Gemini CLI
- Uses Google OAuth
- Model used is reported
- Session summary at end
- "/quit" to exit

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All patterns for all 3 agents are implemented and tested
- [ ] Workflows work end-to-end for all 3 agents
- [ ] Browser automation completes auth for all 3 services
- [ ] Account rotation selects best account across services
- [ ] Field testing confirms reliability in real usage

## Dependencies
- Depends on Phase 1 (Foundation) - patterns, storage
- Depends on Phase 2 (Workflows) - workflow engine, browser skeleton

## Testing Strategy
For each agent:
1. Golden corpus regression tests with captured real output
2. Workflow integration tests with mock panes
3. Manual end-to-end testing with real agent sessions
4. Browser automation tests with recorded sessions

## Open Questions to Resolve
- What undocumented behaviors do each agent have?
- How do we reliably detect session boundaries for Claude Code?
- How does Gemini's usage limit reset work?
- Which auth flows can be fully automated vs need human?","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T08:47:59.851525395Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T08:55:09.109252932Z","closed_at":"2026-01-18T08:55:09.109252932Z","close_reason":"Duplicate of wa-nu4.2 (Phase 3: Full Agent Support). wa-nu4.2 has correct dependency chain. Content merged conceptually.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"wa-tp4","title":"[EPIC] Proactive Suggestions: contextual hints and recommendations","description":"# [EPIC] Proactive Suggestions

## Mission
Provide contextual, helpful suggestions that guide users toward better outcomes without being intrusive.

## Why This Matters
Users often don't know what they could do:
- "What should I check when something fails?"
- "How can I optimize my setup?"
- "What's the next step after this event?"

Proactive suggestions:
- **Guide** users to successful outcomes
- **Educate** about available features
- **Optimize** usage patterns

## Scope

### Contextual Suggestions
Based on current state:
- After event: "Run `wa why` to understand this decision"
- After error: "This often happens when... Try..."
- On status: "Account X is at 85% - consider rotation"

### Suggestion Types
```rust
pub enum SuggestionType {
    NextStep,      // What to do next
    Optimization,  // How to improve
    Warning,       // Upcoming issue
    Tip,           // Useful feature
}

pub struct Suggestion {
    suggestion_type: SuggestionType,
    message: String,
    action: Option<String>,  // Command to run
    learn_more: Option<String>, // Doc link
    priority: Priority,
    context: SuggestionContext,
}
```

### Suggestion Engine
```rust
pub struct SuggestionEngine {
    rules: Vec<SuggestionRule>,
}

impl SuggestionEngine {
    pub fn suggest(&self, context: &Context) -> Vec<Suggestion> {
        self.rules.iter()
            .filter(|r| r.applies(context))
            .map(|r| r.generate(context))
            .collect()
    }
}
```

### Suggestion Rules
- Account at 80%+ usage → suggest rotation
- Repeated rate limits → suggest pacing
- No events detected → suggest pattern check
- First workflow → suggest dry-run
- Error without why → suggest wa why

### Display
- Non-intrusive hints in CLI output
- Optional in status/events
- Dismissable (don't repeat)

## Success Criteria
- Suggestions are helpful, not annoying
- Users discover features through suggestions
- Error recovery is guided

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Suggestion engine implemented
- [ ] Common suggestion rules defined
- [ ] Suggestions appear in relevant contexts
- [ ] Dismissal persisted
- [ ] Not overly intrusive
- [ ] Tests cover suggestion logic

## Testing
- Unit tests for suggestion rules and suppression.
- Integration tests for CLI rendering.
- E2E artifacts include suggestion rule IDs.
